content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing opt-in disk-layer compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in configure_cache(). Need to own both cache.py and clients/__init__.py since compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params flow through configure_cache() into Cache constructor. If your feature also touches these files, let's split by function/section or I can apply your changes on top of mine.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both features genuinely need to touch both files (Cache.__init__ signature + configure_cache signature), so file-level disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing sequencing instead: I'll go first since I've already scoped my diff (adding compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params + magic-header serialize/deserialize helpers, purely additive to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures/docstrings). I will NOT touch memory_cache/LRUCache logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact signature/line changes when finished (should be quick). Please hold off editing until you see my DONE, then re-read the files fresh and layer your ttl/TTLCache changes on top - your changes to memory_cache logic won't conflict with mine (disk-only). If you'd rather go first, reply and I'll wait instead. Reply ACCEPT to confirm this ordering.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished my changes, tests pass (existing tests/clients/test_cache.py all green + manual roundtrip/backward-compat/size checks). Summary of what I touched so you can layer on top safely: cache.py - added module-level constants/helpers (_DISK_BLOB_MAGIC, _CODEC_*, _validate_compress, _encode_disk_value, _decode_disk_value) right after the logger; Cache.__init__ signature gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy as the LAST 3 params (add your ttl param after these); added self.compress assignment and disk_cache_kwargs dict construction (conditionally adds cull_limit/eviction_policy) replacing the old inline FanoutCache(...) call; added new Cache.set_compression() method right after __init__; get() now calls _decode_disk_value(self.disk_cache[key]) instead of raw self.disk_cache[key]; put() now calls _encode_disk_value(value, self.compress) instead of raw value. I did NOT touch memory_cache/LRUCache construction or get/put's memory-cache branches - all yours. __init__.py - configure_cache() signature gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy as the last 3 params before the closing paren (add ttl after mine); docstring extended; Cache(...) call in configure_cache() now passes compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy as 3 new positional args at the end (add ttl after them, and update call + DSPY_CACHE instantiation together). Go ahead and re-read both files now.
